title: Estimate volume (milliliters and liters)
description: Practice estimating the volume of real life objects using milliliters and liters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is closer to the volume of a paint bucket?**


[[☃ radio 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
A single raindrop contains about $1$ milliliter of liquid: 
[[☃ image 1]]

This bottle contains about $1$ liter of liquid: 
[[☃ image 2]]

Can a paint can be filled with about $4$ raindrops or about $4$ liter bottles?
A paint bucket can be filled with about $4$ of the liter bottles.
The volume of a paint bucket is about $4$ liters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is closer to the volume of a glass of lemonade?**

[[☃ radio 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
A single raindrop contains about $1$ milliliter of liquid: 
[[☃ image 1]]

This bottle contains about $1$ liter of liquid:  
[[☃ image 2]]

Can a glass of lemonade be filled with about $500$ raindrops or about $500$ liter bottles?
A glass of lemonade can be filled with about $500$ raindrops.
The volume of a glass of lemonade is about $500$ milliliters.

options.alt: A glass of lemonade with 2 ice cubes, a straw, and a piece of lemon on the edge.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the most reasonable unit to measure the volume of a spoonful of honey.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A single raindrop contains about $1$ milliliter of liquid: 

[[☃ image 1]]

This bottle contains about $1$ liter of liquid:  
[[☃ image 2]]

Which of these is the most reasonable unit to measure the volume of a spoonful of honey?
Milliliters is the most reasonable unit to measure the volume of a spoonful of honey.
